frame_analyses.description: Initially positioning the needle and suture.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle is grasped correctly with the right instrument.

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Approach is steady, placement appears near mark.

frame_analyses.description: Performing first throw of the surgeon's knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw appears secure, knot is formed correctly.

frame_analyses.description: Executing second throw after switching hands.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Left instrument takes over, maintaining tension on suture.

frame_analyses.description: Performing third throw, switching hands again.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right instrument resumes control, securing the knot.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting both ends of the suture inside the trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Precise cutting, careful not to disturb knot integrity.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gap after knot tightening, indicating effective closure.

technique_summary: The trainee demonstrated effective intracorporeal suturing skills, maintaining control and precision throughout. Knot tying was secure, maintaining appropriate hand transitions.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on maintaining hand ergonomics during transitions.
